notes: A 9B model cannot be truly retrained into a 70B model. For a true 70B/72B-class system, start from a real 70B/72B base model and QLoRA/LoRA fine-tune it.
For coding quality, a strong 32B coder model can beat a generic 70B on many programming tasks. Benchmark both before committing weeks of training.

models.why: Strong coding base; cheaper and likely higher coding ROI than generic 70B.
True 70B-class open model; use if you insist on 70B scale.
Strong general model but gated; coding may trail coder-specialized bases.
Useful experiment, but not a true 13B/70B base. Keep as experimental branch.
